reviews.review_description:
My Monsters stands out as a work of art apart: you can feel the inspiration of MOSS, but never copy it. 
This is a unique vision, a living VR diorama where each setting is hand-painted and overflowing with visual poetry.
You play as a hero who teams up with Moti, a small, fragile creature, and this relationship brings a rare emotional depth.
Exploration, puzzles, and combat intertwine naturally, with each level introducing a new mechanic that makes you want to move forward.
The game successfully combines challenge and contemplation, with a dreamlike universe that borders on a coming-of-age tale.
The demo had already impressed me with its emotional intensity, and with the full release, everything suggests that we have a must-have future in the VR catalog, capable of reaching both players seeking adventure and those seeking emotion.
A diorama game the way we love VR, with fun gameplay, puzzles, exploration, and combat!
Complete, beautiful, the first episode in a long series? We can only hope so!

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for this amazing review! 💜
We’re thrilled to hear that My Monsters resonated with you. From Moti and the emotional journey to the hand-painted dreamlike world of Onirium, every part of the game was crafted with love and care.

Your words truly inspire the team!

reviews.review_description:
Meta games don't exactly do narrative and heart in spades. This game is different, and Meta Store needs to do a better job in showcasing games like this.

The game is a platformer, similar to Moss. It is hand painted, and it shows with how vibrant and artistic it looks. The score is moving, but for me, it's the messaging of the game that stands out from the rest.

You control a 'little monster' who is emotionally connected to you. The character is an avatar for our own negative emotions like fear and anxiety, and the game tasks us to help our character through these complex feelings. This is ideal for kids as it helps them understand how to control their negative thoughts and regulate their emotions. Even for an adult like me I found it heartwarming.

There isn't a game out there that does this for kids, so why is it Meta makes this so hard to find? I found out about the game on a comment on a random YT video. Meta Store needs to do better showcasing games like this!

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for your beautiful words. It means a lot to us to know the game’s message reached you that way. We truly believe games can help us reflect and connect with our emotions, and your comment captures that perfectly!

We really appreciate you taking the time to share this and hope more people like you can discover My Monsters.

reviews.review_description:
I'm a fan of this developer's game UNBINARY and I've been looking forward to seeing what they come up with next.

The game is beautiful! I spent some time just gazing around at each environment since there are so many interesting little details. I really like the story so far.

I'm looking forward to seeing how the full release turns out.

This is clearly inspired by Moss and it borrows much of its gameplay design. As a fan of Moss I like the concept, but the execution here is a problem.

The character has a strange "floaty" jump. A quick tap of the jump button or a long press both produce the same height.  The jump seems slow, like the character has no weight. For people used to playing platform games this is going to feel odd.

If the combat starts out at the same difficulty level as the demo it's going to frustrate many gamers who aren't used to playing this style of game.  I suspect the demographic who will buy My Monsters will be primarily casual gamers who won't be expecting to get into a battle like this so early in the game. The combat should start off a little less difficult to ease newcomers into it.  Maybe add Moss's enemy grab feature so you can keep them away?

During that first battle I hit a glitch where my character would no longer attack. It was frozen. I could still move it around but the legs weren't animated. After I got killed and respawned it was fine.

At several points in the game I was unsure how to proceed, and not because I got stuck by a puzzle.

Right at the beginning there was a little card saying "Press X to continue".  Because of the style of the font and the small size of the card the X looked like an A.  I spent a frustrating minute or so pressing the A button while standing in different places thinking I had to be somewhere special to proceed. Eventually I pressed every button and found out it was X. I leaned down close towards the card and saw that it was indeed an X and not an A. 😁

Some of the paths aren't clear and while fumbling through them I got stuck in the scenery a few times. Once that killed my character.

My Monsters has the potential to be a very nice game but it needs more work on the character control so it feels like a solid platformer. 

It also needs a clearer tutorial. For example the first time I tried to open the inventory I couldn't see it because I didn't know it came out of the orb and I had my hands down.

And of course it needs bug fixing, but that's to be expected in a pre release demo.

developer_response.body:
Hey Jioxx,

Thanks so much for the detailed feedback. It was a pleasure to read your thoughts! Your perspective is very important to us, especially the way you described your experience.

The good news is that we've already addressed some of the issues you mentioned. The game's build has progressed, and we’ve made improvements to the character controls and fixed a few known bugs and glitches.

As a fan of UNBINARY and games like Moss, your feedback is very valuable to us, and we look forward to sharing more updates with you soon.

Thanks again for your support!

reviews.review_description:
You are Moti. And you have to find stuff to complete your mission.

Fight against monsters. Jump over abysses. Avoid deadly plants. Move bridges.
Just Stay alive, and bring the stuff that you have found to the guy who needs them. 

Controls aren’t that difficult. 

Very nice game in a hand-painted environment.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for playing and for the kind words! 💛
We’re glad you enjoyed the hand-painted world and the gameplay. We’re working hard to make the adventure even better, and your feedback always keeps us motivated!

reviews.review_description: Everytime i die, the reload screen keeps going and never restarts so i have to close the game each time.

developer_response.body:
We’re really sorry you ran into this issue  and thanks for letting us know!

The good news is that the demo has now been updated to improve performance and stability. If you’d like to give it another try, please make sure you’re playing version 0.9.1021.

Thanks again for the support!
Thank you so much!  We hope you’ll love the full game just as much!

reviews.review_description:
The visuals and story and just the general idea is amazing!! 
But my game keeps crashing (taking me back to the start menu) every time I enter the first encounter with anxiety, and I need to replay everything again =[

developer_response.body:
Sorry to hear about the crash, and thanks for the kind words and the feedback!
This issue was happening in the previous demo version, but we’ve now updated and released a new build that should fix this and improve the overall experience. :)

In the meantime, feel free to join us on Discord to share more details or stay updated: https://discord.gg/JbjaPMYv4Y

reviews.review_title: Fun to play. Theres a bug with meta.
reviews.review_description: No matter what i do, this game always reinstalls itself even when i restart my headset. Its probably not this games fault but it is annoying because i only have 10 gigs left 😬

developer_response.body: Thanks for letting us know! If the issue keeps happening, feel free to reach out to us on our Discord — we’ll do our best to help troubleshoot with you. Appreciate you playing!

reviews.review_description: I loved the design but it does glitch out during positioning. Overall it's actually a good game and I'm looking forward to the full game.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the feedback! We're glad you enjoyed the design, and we invite you to report the positioning glitch with more details on our Discord, so we can investigate further. We appreciate your support and are excited to share the full game with you soon!
https://discord.gg/JbjaPMYv4Y

reviews.review_title: I just got the game when release
reviews.review_description: Yo me what multilayer add please

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review! We don't have any news about multiplayer at the moment, but we really appreciate the suggestion!

reviews.review_description:
The game seems to have potential to be a good and almost equally charming competitor to the Moss titles, though in the demo there were some small issues.

-There was around 2 or 3 moments where my monster got stuck in the environment (usually mid-jump) with one of those times forcing me to restart the level.

- The "combat" needs a lot of work, because as is it was always a bit of a chore to get past, and basically nothing but sloppy button mashing.

But outside of those niggles, I enjoyed what little was there to experience. I loved the look of the game, and mixed with the tone, music, and soft narration; it was like being in a child's story book. 
I do wonder if this game was just completely dropped though, as there is no information on it, it's full release, or anything at all really. Like they made a demo, released it, and just cancelled the project. 😕
 Hopefully something comes of the full release soon.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your feedback! We're sorry to hear about the bug and appreciate you bringing it to our attention. Rest assured, the game is in full development, and we're working hard to deliver the best experience possible.

Follow us on social media for updates and exclusive content, and feel free to join our Discord community to connect with us and other players: https://discord.gg/JbjaPMYv4Y.

Cheers!

reviews.review_title: A charming adventure with My Monsters
reviews.review_description:
I was genuinely impressed by this demo! The graphics are truly unique and create such a magical atmosphere.
If you enjoy games like Moss, you'll love this experience. Interacting with the monster is delightful, and exploring the world is a lot of fun. I can’t wait for the full release to uncover more of the world’s secrets!
The game’s theme is captivating and allows for a real connection with the character.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the kind words! It's awesome to know that you felt that connection with Moti!

reviews.review_description: My Monsters is an enchanting VR experience that truly captivates the imagination. The hand-painted art style creates a stunningly beautiful world that feels both surreal and inviting. As you journey through the dream-like realm of Onirium, the game's deep narrative touches on profound themes of anxiety, fear, and loneliness, making it relatable on many levels. Teaming up with Moti, the adorable little monster, not only brings a sense of companionship but also encourages players to engage with their emotions and foster empathy. The combination of dynamic combat, intricate puzzles, and a heartfelt story makes My Monsters is a unique adventure that promises moments of self-discovery and personal growth. I can’t wait to see how the game evolves! (FYI: from the devs of Unbinary) YT: @misschiefvr

developer_response.body:
Hey MissChief, I'm sure Moti was thrilled to read your review! Thank you so much for your kind words and for the great video you created on your YT channel!

Moti's role in fostering connection and empathy is something we’re passionate about, and we’re thrilled that you connected with the story. Thank you for being part of the journey!

reviews.review_description: My Monsters is a game with an incredible artistic direction, very unique compared to other VR games I've played. It creates an immersive journey through humanity's inner monsters, something I believe everyone can relate to. I have no doubt that the final product will be a remarkable experience, especially from an artistic standpoint!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for your thoughtful review! We’re thrilled to hear that the artistic direction and theme resonated with you. Creating that immersive experience around the inner monsters is so important to us, and feedback like yours keeps us inspired!

reviews.review_description: Pretty cool game. I like the cool vr platforming and I especially like the art.

developer_response.body: Thanks, Legobrickstorm! Glad you liked it—means a lot to us!

reviews.review_description:
My Monsters is an action-adventure VR game that with a 100% hand-painted art, creating a visually stunning world full of fantasy and surrealism! It’s absolutely amazing! 

At the heart of the game is a deep narrative that explores humanity's greatest inner struggles—anxiety, fear, and loneliness! Im sure we can all relate to this at some point in our lives! As a player, you are transported to Onirium, a dream-like realm where you meet Moti, a frightened little monster with no memory ( so cute ). 

You’re never alone! Team up with your inner monster, Moti, to face a variety of enemies, solve intricate puzzles, and overcome challenges in a shared journey. This partnership mechanic fosters a fantastic & powerful connection with Moti, requiring empathy and courage to navigate obstacles and reveal fragments of a meaningful dreamlike tale. Every stage introduces new aspects of this bond, allowing players to experience moments of self-discovery and emotional depth.

I recommend this to be in your games library I’ve had a blast playing it! Well done devs stunning game.

developer_response.body:
Hey Tom! We’re delighted you enjoyed the game’s art direction and themes! Crafting a world that feels both immersive and meaningful was our goal, and it’s so rewarding to know you felt that connection. 

We can’t wait to show even more in the full release—thanks for the kind words and support!

reviews.review_description: A beautiul journey that engages and challenges the mind.

developer_response.body: Thank you for sharing your thoughts! We’re so happy to hear that My Monsters provided an engaging and stimulating experience for you. It’s great to know that our game is leaving a positive impression, and we can’t wait to share more with you!
